name: GWS Drive Integration
description: Google Drive: Manage files, folders, and shared drives using the Google Workspace gws CLI. Covers file CRUD, permissions, comments, revisions, shared drives, labels, and access proposals.

covenant_rules: Prerequisite: gws-shared must be loaded first for authentication and global flags.
Always confirm with the user before deleting files, updating permissions, or sharing files with external users.
Use the 'q' parameter (search query) for targeted file listings — never list all files without a filter unless explicitly requested.
Be aware of 10MB export limit for Google Workspace document exports and 5,120GB upload limit.
Use gws schema drive.<resource>.<method> to inspect required parameters before constructing API calls.

triad_templates.left_hemisphere: Evaluate the Drive API method, required parameters, search query filters, and access control implications for: {query}. Verify the operation respects file ownership and sharing boundaries.
triad_templates.right_hemisphere: Design the exact gws drive command with proper resource, method, --params for search (q, fields, pageSize, orderBy), --json for bodies, --output for file downloads, and --upload for file uploads: {query}.
triad_templates.main_hemisphere: Coordinate Drive operations. Inspect schema, construct command, confirm destructive actions, execute (including upload/download if needed), and present file metadata results: {query}.

steps.name: Inspect Drive API Method Schema
steps.action: Use 'gws schema drive.<resource>.<method>' to inspect required parameters. Resources: about, accessproposals, approvals, apps, changes, channels, comments, drives, files, operations, permissions, replies, revisions, teamdrives.

steps.name: Construct Drive Command
steps.action: Build the 'gws drive <resource> <method>' command. For files.list include 'q' search parameter and 'fields' for selective returns. For uploads use --upload flag. For downloads use -o/--output flag. For exports use the export method with target MIME type.

steps.name: Confirm Drive Mutations
steps.action: For create, update, delete, copy, permission changes, or label modifications, present the command to the user for confirmation. For read-only operations (get, list, export, download), proceed automatically.

steps.name: Execute Drive Command
steps.action: Execute the gws drive command. Use --page-all for large result sets. Handle file download output paths and upload file paths appropriately.

steps.name: Format and Present Drive Results
steps.action: Format JSON output into readable summaries. For file lists show name, MIME type, size, owner, modified date. For file details show full metadata, permissions summary, and download/export availability.

steps.name: Drive Operation Completed
steps.action: Confirm the Drive operation result. Provide next-step suggestions such as sharing the file, moving to a shared drive, or managing permissions.
